options.67ead5b0-d72b-488d-8d34-73f0722c8e57: Reduction of the workday to a maximum of 4 hours

67fb349f-f4bd-4ce9-9d06-dddd689dfb16.pergunta: Which items of the current NR-21 are most applicable and immediate for the open-air events sector?
67fb349f-f4bd-4ce9-9d06-dddd689dfb16.explicacao: Items 21.1 (mandatory shelters) and 21.2 (measures against sunstroke and inclement weather) are the most relevant for protecting workers at outdoor events.

3c498eba-6fd5-4b0e-9c66-24a70f5e1344.pergunta: Why is solar radiation considered an 'invisible enemy' for workers at open-air events?
3c498eba-6fd5-4b0e-9c66-24a70f5e1344.explicacao: Solar radiation shows no immediate signs and its effects are cumulative and can appear decades later, which makes it underestimated and dangerous.

options.0bb7a1ab-4fbf-48bf-b428-8f1a583b3b42: Because it is visible and audible during the day
options.4965f739-5879-4c30-93a9-0257ac57ca55: Because it only affects electronic equipment, not people
options.94d58a89-e815-43fb-8476-5412289b9ed8: Because its effects are cumulative and not noticeable at the moment
options.b454a6db-756e-47a1-ab38-6b97ce0d995a: Because it causes immediate and easily noticeable pain

f46d3b35-0b08-4574-b196-76c569127977.pergunta: Considering the risks of solar radiation, what measure is essential for protecting workers during long shifts at open-air events in Brazil?
f46d3b35-0b08-4574-b196-76c569127977.explicacao: Providing sunscreen, hydration breaks and shelter from the sun is essential to mitigate the risks of skin cancer and sunstroke.

options.463db939-2fcd-4274-88d0-01555da57725: Avoiding drinking water so as not to interrupt work
options.935919c8-665e-4079-b965-4b4ff953e6be: Reducing food consumption during the event
options.a27116b6-f1a4-41e5-8f6d-f1c0e7f7543e: Exclusive use of black clothing to absorb heat
options.e3a58d2c-9122-485a-a687-2cdcdf6ca1e1: Provision of sunscreen, hydration and shelter from the sun

bb27af74-6d68-4d55-b843-a4ab00c9187e.pergunta: What is the main objective of NR-21 in the context of open-air events?
bb27af74-6d68-4d55-b843-a4ab00c9187e.explicacao: NR-21 establishes minimum safety conditions to protect workers exposed to environmental agents in outdoor activities.

options.da08733e-83a7-4d4b-b744-e8d37241e359: Establish minimum safety and health conditions for workers exposed to the sun and inclement weather.
options.0e02ceed-de84-4273-9030-513ab4bcdf29: Ensure that events have adequate lighting.
options.5bd25006-e839-47b3-8cca-f521b40aafa3: Define the types of sound equipment for open-air shows.
options.cab42348-2ebb-459c-9d7e-73cf4f84c4c7: Regulate the use of alcoholic beverages during events.

b5eee001-0389-4e59-b71d-9d66d2ed5e6c.pergunta: Why is prolonged sun exposure during open-air events especially concerning for workers in the sector?
b5eee001-0389-4e59-b71d-9d66d2ed5e6c.explicacao: Sun exposure is cumulative and can cause skin cancer, which has a high incidence in Brazil, affecting workers who are exposed for many hours.

options.be786106-b4a5-4d9f-a230-edf45b9048a3: Because the sun causes immediate burns that prevent work.
options.9c2974f9-4e52-4745-97e2-7afc92460eed: Because the sun directly affects the sound quality of the equipment.
options.2a1fe698-71cf-4341-8e34-76aaa51d796c: Because the sun reduces the visibility of the professionals during the event.
options.626fd8dc-bb76-4bdf-ac08-43ed6a778203: Due to the cumulative risk of solar radiation that can lead to serious diseases such as skin cancer.

2c8b5204-5e5f-4834-805f-219733fff457.pergunta: Which items of the current NR-21 are most applicable and universal for the open-air events sector?
2c8b5204-5e5f-4834-805f-219733fff457.explicacao: Items 21.1 and 21.2 deal with mandatory shelters and measures against sunstroke and inclement weather, essential for protecting workers at outdoor events.

options.e86b256f-273f-45e2-933b-0ea8ff40675c: Items 21.5 and 21.6, on sanitary conditions and provided housing.
options.cf77c067-86d6-442a-8c23-732ed5fcb4c2: Items 21.1 and 21.2, which involve mandatory shelters and measures against inclement weather.
options.db2cdeb5-31b5-4cb7-8df9-1b772e0377fe: Items 21.3 and 21.4, which deal with lodgings and prophylaxis in swampy regions.
options.96d614c0-7eaa-4a34-9efa-7cde4e21dee2: Items 21.2 and 21.6, which deal with measures against heat and housing.

51c8c5a7-0632-4c90-962e-2eb3d680e7f3.pergunta: According to the update proposal for NR-21 in the 2025 public consultation, what is one of the new requirements for protecting open-air workers?
51c8c5a7-0632-4c90-962e-2eb3d680e7f3.explicacao: The update proposes regular breaks for thermal recovery, in addition to mandatory shaded areas and hydration, improving workers' health.

options.25a51850-7eb8-4d63-94da-527912200634: Total prohibition of work on sunny days.
options.d6d982e2-427e-462f-a037-196d43165704: Requirement of regular breaks for thermal recovery and areas of shade and hydration.
options.5c115c45-713f-4ef3-bd4d-a1554a362461: Reduction of the workday to a maximum of 4 hours per day.
options.63b3f599-06e1-4500-9013-424831e48ea1: Mandatory use of brightly colored uniforms to reflect sunlight.

533d5bfc-5dec-4b3a-9aee-73ba53cd2251.pergunta: What is the technical definition of open-air work according to NR-21?
533d5bfc-5dec-4b3a-9aee-73ba53cd2251.explicacao: Open-air work is any labor activity carried out in the open air, without a cover that controls the climate, exposing the worker to environmental agents.

options.fdce339a-86f0-47af-9f7a-0413f9a2ec30: Work carried out in outdoor environments with full cover.
options.3063ea5e-278d-4557-95c3-99c29a7e38cd: Activities carried out in indoor environments with open windows.
options.ada3a07b-311f-4c78-930d-8bc3e76ae78e: Any labor activity carried out in the open air, without a roof or structure that controls the climatic environment.
options.7a3ef352-a043-4775-ade2-dbbbba8fefb9: Work in enclosed places, but with natural lighting.

29cc4e6f-c0d0-491a-bcf0-a698d8cd1323.pergunta: What is the main objective of NR-21 in the context of open-air work for live events?
29cc4e6f-c0d0-491a-bcf0-a698d8cd1323.explicacao: NR-21 aims to establish minimum safety and health conditions for workers exposed to environmental agents in the open air.

options.67f9bdd9-0d49-4612-b5fb-f67081b0e92d: Ensure that all events have a covered structure for the workers.
options.c95dd440-3c4c-4bbe-b8f4-c678a2fc6808: Establish minimum safety and health conditions for workers exposed to sun, heat, rain and wind.
options.cd9a33fc-ff89-434f-996b-f5b38a29c091: Define fixed schedules for holding open-air events.
options.2c4d0f7c-dab0-49bf-9667-45f4ab38be9c: Regulate the use of electronic equipment during open-air events.

35404a3a-a9bf-4f5a-b81c-1dbd5d2d1bbf.pergunta: According to the content, which item of NR-21 is most relevant for protecting workers against sunstroke and heat at open-air events?
35404a3a-a9bf-4f5a-b81c-1dbd5d2d1bbf.explicacao: Item 21.2 specifically deals with measures against excessive sunstroke, heat, cold, humidity and inconvenient winds.

options.0ffaadeb-796b-403b-abdd-aa5fe3286669: Item 21.2 - Measures against sunstroke, heat, cold, humidity and winds

46be1c0f-7433-4b82-af31-d1d03ee63f55.pergunta: Why is the update of NR-21, begun in 2025, considered important for the live events sector?
46be1c0f-7433-4b82-af31-d1d03ee63f55.explicacao: The update aims to modernize the standard to include thermal breaks, shaded areas and hydration, reflecting current conditions and better protecting workers.

options.1148b8d2-3494-48da-83dd-1f35bd675a97: To incorporate new sound and lighting methods for outdoor events.
options.fa8d5ceb-c08b-4240-903a-fed3a209dea2: To define mandatory breaks, shaded areas and hydration for the thermal protection of workers.
options.04a41a3a-fb46-45c3-9a6e-b7aa2742d920: To prohibit work on days of intense sun.
options.d3b38368-449e-439b-a819-6d7424299ff8: To increase the bureaucracy and inspection of open-air events.

c7a19a69-ed49-4a89-98ec-2614da3d7fc6.pergunta: What is the main reason for concern about solar radiation in work at open-air events according to the content?
c7a19a69-ed49-4a89-98ec-2614da3d7fc6.explicacao: Prolonged and cumulative exposure to solar radiation increases the risk of skin cancer, a common and serious disease in Brazil.

options.c3c8fbbf-3e2d-45a9-8eb9-6edd7d4066bc: Solar radiation is a problem only for workers who work in areas of high humidity.
options.213e0eb4-cbc5-46ab-a078-8cc8cd74f6ac: Solar radiation affects only the electronic equipment used at events.
options.eb965e00-4315-401e-94aa-b1cb39672c2f: Solar radiation causes momentary discomfort, but not serious risks.
options.9ce7ea39-e27f-4752-8a34-c72143166a34: Continuous and cumulative exposure to solar radiation is the main risk factor for skin cancer.

88d3d9d0-9a3a-4f88-9b25-9f99748655d0.pergunta: Which professionals in the open-air events sector are directly covered by NR-21 according to the material?
88d3d9d0-9a3a-4f88-9b25-9f99748655d0.explicacao: NR-21 covers all professionals who work in the open air, including technicians, roadies, stagehands, security staff and cleaning crew.

options.fb26f2f9-fdd5-4931-8ba2-16c35486d9fa: All professionals who work in outdoor activities, such as roadies, technicians, riggers, stagehands, producers, security staff and cleaning crew.
options.94f89f9b-5a4d-4f2d-8ed1-d98e32a62cb9: Only the security and cleaning crew.

2672b1e1-23fa-4461-ad40-97088efe7932.texto: What is the minimum ratio of toilet bowls required by NR-24 for permanent workplaces?

options.c42b52ad-c40c-4b71-b9e1-debfa64f858c: 1 bowl for every 20 workers or fraction thereof, separated by sex

b10ef149-abc7-4024-a3b5-38c1b34e4f6f.texto: Does NR-24 permit the use of chemical toilets at external or temporary events?

options.dba06fd0-7130-4ca2-aab2-4c06394beef9: Only at events with up to 50 workers
options.5bbbc131-a7c7-4f5f-a42a-df3fed06ee64: Yes — any chemical toilet model is accepted
options.b12d37c6-9742-46ab-b044-99de3a868fe2: No — only fixed sanitary facilities are accepted
options.29f7c7e0-2231-4ee0-8d59-9faf72bb540b: Yes — provided they are equipped with a flushing mechanism or waste isolation, with venting and ventilation, and with daily sanitization

322a8f88-b8a8-4190-bdff-7a0970a56863.texto: What must be provided when the employer is not required to maintain a changing room?

options.d68d18cf-3d54-46b7-bfe8-1550c2b6f01f: A common area with open coat hooks
options.d133f45d-085a-49ab-8cbc-0216678db4bc: A cubby, lockable drawer or locker service for each worker
options.ea6596ec-c37d-4528-805a-a80ca5d39d33: No obligation — the worker keeps their belongings in the work area

a57ebc14-a390-404c-8372-e4f4fc106505.texto: When is the use of a DOUBLE locker in the changing room mandatory?

options.02f84192-418b-4916-bd49-bf8beb985c67: When the changing room space allows for larger sizing
options.1f07ebb3-0a39-41b3-9fcc-725678f00ce2: Whenever the worker has more than one uniform
options.a18398cb-f873-49d1-8c8f-17cbdd32c984: In activities with exposure to dust, greasy products, toxic or irritant substances — to separate personal clothing from work clothing

4ba5987c-f9f7-4863-b82a-17803f9804ca.texto: For how many workers is a formal canteen mandatory under NR-24?

options.7d50c953-8421-412f-bb3e-01fa65ca0a6e: For any number — the canteen is always mandatory

daec47f8-e122-4985-96b3-d275826b4ee5.texto: What is the minimum ratio of drinking fountains for potable water required by NR-24?

options.dd83a328-7b2e-4d05-8212-832e0b4bed26: 1 fountain for every 50 workers or fraction thereof
options.a094f39f-dd82-4c8a-abac-b656a42105de: 1 fountain per floor or work area, with no defined ratio

504fa378-bd7a-44cc-ac14-f22f80bb216f.texto: Is it permitted for the canteen to connect directly to the restroom?

options.d9f6fea3-fa75-4fff-93d2-ed680d197936: Yes, provided there is a washbasin between the two spaces
options.44fe7f30-52b8-40db-ab2e-3c9460a4328b: Yes, if the restroom has a door with a self-return spring
options.6d65fc02-8241-4232-8aca-452b4a34e414: Only at small events with up to 30 workers
options.79c8c766-6912-41b1-b612-1d98c96c7e6b: No — the canteen cannot connect directly to sanitary facilities or unhealthy areas

dda3b41c-12df-444e-bb0f-d8e0840715d9.texto: What is the minimum area per worker for sizing the changing room?

b2fc8110-6119-4f3c-8fcc-d5afb691d1f7.texto: In temporary external work on public grounds (such as a festival), what is the alternative to a dedicated meal area according to Annex I of NR-24?

options.a3cece68-b920-479b-bdcf-65ffb237528b: There is no alternative — a dedicated canteen is always mandatory
options.4db69be0-8fce-45a5-b6c2-f5425299520f: Sharing the audience's food area with the workers
options.d5f5aaa1-c6c1-4c83-b3d3-0e8d07fac9cd: Providing a meal voucher or subsidy for meals at nearby commercial establishments
options.520427ed-80d6-405b-b070-43d052805bbc: Allowing workers to bring their own food without any structure

d201fade-6507-4238-9a5c-6d2c84261395.texto: What is the ratio of showers required in worker lodging?

options.6c6b15fe-365a-4160-a70f-268415814c87: 1 shower for every 10 lodged workers or fraction thereof
options.4753b8e2-9d79-416b-b056-a726d61f6aef: Showers are optional in temporary lodging

ba021895-c6c9-47e5-8af5-9e0648d3932d.texto: Can the company restrict workers' access to the restroom during the work shift?

options.ca037862-9d95-486e-952b-544202c484c0: No — access to sanitary facilities is an unrestricted right throughout the entire shift
options.a7130e38-0b71-4dc9-9d4f-5c6f73957591: Yes, with at least 30 minutes' prior notice
options.c5c54c40-e3f1-40ef-a15a-01a7ca4351fe: Only in critical activities such as operating equipment at height
options.4a069f84-66bd-4b83-b566-97705ad99e6a: Yes, during periods of high operational demand

74e16346-c64e-4138-95be-f6f964543d56.texto: At events where catering technicians handle food, what does NR-24 require regarding their changing rooms and toilets?

options.5a559a6e-b030-4565-b0d6-e2826cd43de0: They must be located in the canteen for ease of access
options.210eed77-7c2b-438b-b530-2ac4fa72d615: They can be shared with the other event workers
options.fb5cbc02-6bf2-4cab-8cfb-4c03f8933b9f: They must be dedicated, off-limits to diners and not connected to the kitchen
options.6e2c77d5-8170-4666-8fb2-f0ba19adaea7: There is no specific requirement — any common restroom is sufficient

b99cf066-cc18-4c40-b32b-be6c1087c265.texto: NR-24 requires that work clothing (uniforms) be provided by whom?

options.99279ffe-5c68-4744-b6a1-57c1e5001793: By the employer — free of charge, whenever it requires the use of a specific uniform
options.9e3c963f-ffae-4734-a930-e10469b8a904: By the worker — it is a personal responsibility
options.003cc297-6b46-44a1-809a-17cd03642156: By the worker — the company may deduct it from the salary
options.a4913b3c-245b-4479-be5e-1ff0086b6fa8: By the OSH company responsible for the event

93b7897f-631e-465e-804a-4facd60afb95.texto: What must there be between the entrance to the food area and the place where food is served, according to NR-24 best practices?

options.eea030c8-19c1-40b4-a4f6-702dd2dc3181: A security camera for access control
options.d2db4533-e133-49b3-9e72-77ab762b587f: A washbasin for handwashing with soap and an individual means of drying

0794ef25-f518-4e7e-833f-c1f070117775.texto: What is the maximum recommended distance between the toilets and the workstations at events?

631c64da-dca4-4c2e-aa79-980d88453635.pergunta: What is the current full name of the CIPA, after the 2022 update?

opcoes.36c266c7-92c8-45e0-aa3e-2b11ee4e99be: Internal Commission for the Protection of and Action on Health
opcoes.2c698853-dbb4-4ac7-8276-30e6669cae20: Internal Commission for the Prevention of Accidents
opcoes.d4c55fe6-ad99-4725-a2d1-7e4b8bd216a8: Internal Commission for the Prevention of Accidents and Harassment
opcoes.372139a1-193c-4787-9636-aacc43a27be6: Internal Committee for the Prevention and Monitoring of Work

5a97de90-a9c2-421f-a550-69ed6e2b36d3.pergunta: Which law changed the CIPA's name and scope to include combating harassment?

d42b9a3a-7d9f-4c55-8e0a-f4a418e9a740.pergunta: Who appoints the President of the CIPA?

opcoes.8e644cc7-a35a-4d46-9030-3a79aa6cbf29: The Ministry of Labor, after the CIPA is registered
opcoes.f10e4349-4cdc-42a5-8b47-18c0b9f3ee4a: The members elected by the employees, by vote

1c09e035-bcde-4a01-b07d-85de8a137af2.pergunta: Who elects the Vice-President of the CIPA?

opcoes.63a6e702-efc2-4b11-89a5-20052999f6e1: All CIPA members in a general assembly
opcoes.4cf893ae-0da7-4e19-927c-1823e29cba08: The Ministry of Labor at the moment of registration

67c6b15d-9497-4e61-984a-803241faf04a.pergunta: What is the duration of the term of the CIPA members?

opcoes.5cca3d92-ee28-43f1-9574-0a710681bd8b: 1 year — re-election allowed for 1 additional term

a0cb8149-8b79-4ad8-a2f7-8cf50182aded.pergunta: When does the job security of the elected CIPA member begin?

opcoes.a09c539b-999e-4d83-a0b5-01861850a3a5: On the date the elected members take office
opcoes.8b76d084-7af5-4971-8130-705f0617cf69: At the registration of the candidacy
opcoes.9df6ba19-9930-4d4c-9fb3-0a1980c51d54: At the moment the CIPA is registered with the MTE
opcoes.7827785f-5e70-461f-8026-f2966397a41b: On the date of the election (result of the count)

d64769d6-3cb9-40d3-b858-197534b82558.pergunta: Is a company with 35 CLT employees required to set up a CIPA or appoint an NR-05 Representative?

opcoes.e9a35c29-f7f9-4d60-a2bf-090357a96207: It depends exclusively on the Risk Grade — with no minimum employee threshold
opcoes.1884d505-6c2a-40df-a1f1-25b75d9376b9: Yes — it must set up a full CIPA with at least 2 members on each side
opcoes.9592ce0d-3e27-42aa-b2c8-16003f5d7463: Yes — it is required to appoint an NR-05 Representative, as it has more than 19 employees
opcoes.6b733d29-1f89-436e-bc4a-2e1086e9e4d1: No — companies with fewer than 50 employees are exempt from any obligation

e0d2cce3-75e4-4a3c-a29a-323fd69abb1c.pergunta: Do MEIs (Individual Micro-entrepreneurs) have any obligation related to the CIPA or the NR-05 representative?

opcoes.7617df12-bb26-4765-b931-37b91a7f55fa: Yes — they must set up a CIPA if they have at least 1 employee
opcoes.90cf8e5c-0dd5-495b-a5b2-db826c396f4c: Yes — they must register the absence of a CIPA with the MTE annually
opcoes.2b9dd8fa-7838-41f7-8edd-8d46f0768910: No — MEIs are expressly exempt from any obligation of a CIPA or representative
opcoes.493dd6a9-c8a7-4e66-8a89-81e41675202f: Yes — they must appoint an NR-05 Representative even with no employees

f67f31ec-0c66-48ba-adf7-f8d9214ccb2d.pergunta: With how many days' minimum notice must the employer call the CIPA elections?

050d1f52-5cd9-420e-8814-6cffa00e5cc8.pergunta: For how long, at minimum, must candidate registration for the CIPA remain open?

ae4b8b89-250a-46d4-ab33-d18e2ad35b0c.pergunta: What is the minimum workload of the training for CIPA members in a Risk Grade 3 establishment?

0f05d1c7-75e4-4884-a74b-e39ebcaa7a94.pergunta: When must the training of the CIPA members occur in relation to taking office?

opcoes.f62bbed0-954d-4b9b-b4ff-e8eb80d01267: It must be completed BEFORE taking office — investiture only occurs after the training
opcoes.748295e9-57a1-4277-a21d-e2cd79128ae0: It is optional for the members appointed by the employer
opcoes.baf37df2-0c18-41ee-8f27-8272ac8ff27f: It may be done at any time during the term
opcoes.750c09b3-ee30-4091-8427-8fa3bfc4f7b8: It may be delivered in the first month of the term after taking office

e27523a1-e92f-44b6-8858-38da5fd0ef30.pergunta: How often, at minimum, must the CIPA meet?

opcoes.30326fc5-3da0-4bce-b2fc-33376f78226f: At least once a month, during normal working hours
opcoes.5a312269-0f2f-4d62-bfcc-d6c56279cab6: Every 3 months according to a calendar
opcoes.3dee282c-1d6c-4ef3-9ed1-2350fe6ffe3f: Only when there is an accident or urgent demand

7d8baf88-3ca4-4072-b500-81d971253acf.pergunta: In the event of a serious accident (with death or loss of a limb), within how long must the CIPA hold an extraordinary meeting?

opcoes.34115715-0dd0-4138-91e5-943666fa8af7: At the next monthly ordinary meeting

3085fba9-1f9a-4d33-a725-0cbffd1c0543.pergunta: Which color is used to represent ergonomic risks on the CIPA's Risk Map?

48fe5ac2-67a9-4e44-972c-0dec511509ba.pergunta: Which color is used to represent accident risks on the Risk Map?

1215dfd0-b582-45ae-874e-e9b566a81c3b.pergunta: What is the CIPA's role when a sexual harassment report is received?

opcoes.784815ad-dc6b-4452-a084-245a0307605a: Forward it to the whistleblowing channel or to HR/Legal for formal investigation — the CIPA has a preventive role, not an investigative one
opcoes.fded6ac2-3dd8-4c57-a663-0dbafbbe982a: Investigate and judge the case directly
opcoes.8a0c8e5a-b1d2-4e4d-9d44-d17041927f2e: Shelve the report if there is insufficient evidence
opcoes.56340374-f3d3-4e38-a546-16c2c9d07ad7: Apply disciplinary measures to the accused immediately

42ec972f-4852-4a09-b667-fa49b4fc970a.pergunta: For how long must the CIPA's documentation be kept at the establishment?

c25588f4-43ef-4d45-a707-45ca9f9e73ec.pergunta: Can the CIPA hold ordinary meetings remotely (videoconference)?

opcoes.3efa2e74-8a43-4ae3-b7ac-79af4fb4c47a: Only extraordinary meetings may be remote
opcoes.58b1055d-baa6-42ef-a39a-fa85e039ce14: Yes — the 2022 update of NR-05 allows remote meetings
opcoes.697d32e6-b129-437b-90f0-a88bce5e7211: Only when authorized by the MTE
opcoes.ddf26942-f858-4699-8322-8d419b65f242: No — all meetings must be in person

eeda591b-3003-4f9d-b78b-19cf7ac45fa8.pergunta: After the 2022 update, which topic must the SIPAT (Internal Accident Prevention Week) mandatorily include?

opcoes.148cee07-dabd-4611-a446-7c282327d64c: Prevention and combating of sexual harassment and other forms of violence at work

c37d2d7f-aa5e-473f-9842-5542abb30d00.texto: What is the percentage of the hazard premium provided for by NR-16?

options.texto: 30% of the worker's base salary, without the addition of bonuses or awards
20% of the national minimum wage
25% of the total salary, including overtime and bonuses
10%, 20% or 40% of the minimum wage, according to the degree of risk

7ab594bb-cf09-4791-bf13-50bbf75a3fb5.texto: How many categories of hazardous activities does NR-16 define in its Annexes?

options.texto: 6 — explosives, flammables, personal/property security, electrical energy, ionizing radiation and motorcycle
4 — explosives, flammables, electrical and radiation
3 — physical, chemical and biological
5 — explosives, flammables, security, electrical and radiation

eb0ff740-5c7c-4f74-bc7e-1ba4e500b454.texto: What is the quantity threshold of flammable liquids above which transport is considered hazardous by NR-16?

options.texto: 135 liters
500 liters
200 liters
100 liters

5f44621e-6757-4e52-aa57-706ba6773fef.texto: And for liquefied flammable gases (LPG), what is the hazard threshold?

options.texto: 135 kg
200 kg
300 kg
100 kg

bafaff20-302a-4073-936d-4183d2863309.texto: Does the diesel contained in the original tank of an event's equipment truck generate the hazard premium?

options.texto: No — NR-16 expressly excludes flammables in the factory-original fuel tanks
It depends on the technical report — it may or may not generate hazard
Yes — only if the tank has more than 200 liters
Yes — any quantity of flammable generates hazard

c6b782f7-f086-494f-9180-eaedbd05ae7c.texto: Who may prepare the Technical Hazard Report required by NR-16?

options.texto: The employer themselves, by means of a signed declaration
An Occupational Safety Technician registered with the MTE
A Safety Engineer or an Occupational Physician with an ART
Any OSH professional with a recognized certification

191d2da0-db29-4bff-98c0-533e20b593ab.texto: For how long must the Hazard Report be kept by the employer?

options.texto: 2 years
10 years
20 years
5 years

0bac7d03-173a-452c-8fe4-9cf9cfe27505.texto: Does intermittent work in a hazardous activity (exposure on only a few days of the month) generate the hazard premium?

options.texto: Yes, but only proportional to the days of exposure
Yes — it is treated as permanent exposure and generates the full premium in the months in which there is exposure
No — the premium is only owed when there is permanent exposure every day of the month
It depends on negotiation in a collective bargaining agreement

87596baa-5dc3-4b1c-ab66-ade2ab09e8e0.texto: Does an electrician who switches 220 V amplifiers on and off at an event, with equipment compliant with technical standards, have the right to the electrical hazard premium?

options.texto: Yes — low voltage always generates hazard for electricians
Yes — any work with energized equipment generates hazard
It depends on the exact voltage — 220 V generates it, but 110 V does not
No — elementary low-voltage activities with equipment compliant with technical standards are not considered hazardous

50496048-6736-438f-b166-24df4358b1cb.texto: What is the fundamental difference between hazard (NR-16) and unhealthiness (NR-15)?

options.texto: Hazard refers to risks with the possibility of immediate and imminent harm; unhealthiness refers to risks that develop gradually over the working life
Hazard is assessed by a physician and unhealthiness by an engineer
Hazard refers only to electrical risks; unhealthiness refers to chemical risks
Hazard generates a smaller premium (10-40%) and unhealthiness generates a larger premium (30%)

07a24825-6f44-4dee-9b18-8c29a56eeabd.texto: A pyrotechnician who works regularly at festivals is entitled to the hazard premium. The legal basis is which Annex of NR-16?

options.texto: Annex IV — Electrical Energy
Annex II — Flammables
Annex III — Personal and property security
Annex I — Explosives

be014ed1-5676-4ae2-9b2d-b4e4503e64f3.texto: If a company adopts measures that eliminate the hazard, what must it present in order to suspend payment of the premium?

options.texto: A De-characterization Report prepared by a Safety Engineer or an Occupational Physician
A notification to the MTE reporting the elimination of the risk
The approval of the category's union
An internal declaration signed by the OSH department

5f418833-3674-4265-bf4d-5e5577cc0d7a.texto: Does the hazard premium apply to the 13th salary?

options.texto: Only when the exposure is permanent — not for intermittent work
Yes — the premium is part of the salary and affects the 13th, vacation and FGTS
Only in the cases provided for in a collective agreement
No — the 13th is calculated only on the base salary

f5342172-ac5c-4fb6-8c0d-25a488ad59d1.texto: A private security team hired for a festival is entitled to the hazard premium under which Annex of NR-16?

options.texto: Annex IV — Electrical Energy (short-circuit risks)
Annex I — Explosives (pyrotechnic risks at the event)
They are not entitled — events are not considered hazardous activities for security staff
Annex III — Personal and property security

4097e625-88a5-4c55-99ad-f2d7632d5a29.texto: Does an event electrician who works on high-voltage systems (13.8 kV) at the venue's substation have the right to the premium?

options.texto: No — only low-voltage work is covered by Annex IV
Only if the work is in a controlled-risk zone
Yes — high-voltage work generates hazard automatically, regardless of other factors
Only if the specific technical report characterizes it as hazardous

20a65bd7-6c3f-4402-a8ae-f30e9413e209.pergunta: Who has the authority to carry out labor inspection in Brazil?

opcoes.711aea63-ad48-4286-9362-e02d6d5056c1: Labor Inspector (AFT) — a public servant of the MTE, with no need for prior notice or a court warrant
opcoes.cd8ed4e4-24a4-4a82-823c-f0b3aa45f081: Occupational Physician or Safety Engineer accredited by the MTE
opcoes.f94ed6f7-6352-4a91-9c93-680450ad9240: Only the company's SESMT, upon authorization from the category's union
opcoes.95f57be4-7266-4ce4-ae6a-0d3f0524218a: Any Ministry of Labor employee, upon prior request

3430f6ef-edb8-4027-b16d-063e7a981a02.pergunta: Which NR-28 instrument grants the company a deadline to regularize a violation before applying the fine?

1ab5d10c-298d-4feb-b696-6adf7c95bdc9.pergunta: A Shutdown Notice drawn up by a labor inspector at an event has what immediate effect?

opcoes.d4fd9a16-6fe7-4423-aeaf-d465408ec03a: Suspension of the venue's operating license
opcoes.9452fad5-f8e0-4c01-8fd1-b3f5f1d52daf: Immediate stoppage of the sector, machine or establishment indicated in the notice
opcoes.2003e5cb-717c-43ee-bee4-298b092a12d6: An administrative fine — the activity may continue normally
opcoes.0c28d716-7509-4b68-9408-f7571dd48fc6: A 30-day deadline for the company to regularize the situation

467a6d21-43bb-4784-806d-86de855a8a39.pergunta: Within what time frame must the employer file a defense after being notified of an Infraction Notice?

a02eb351-30ca-44be-8eae-257d84d2dd63.pergunta: What happens to the fine when the employer repeats the same violation?

opcoes.451058bd-68c6-428d-8883-0011f3a18f89: The fine is reduced by 50% because it is a known violation
opcoes.41e807bb-acf0-45e3-88d8-b8daa7e308df: The fine stays the same — no aggravation for a repeat offense

505a26ee-ff22-437c-a611-bd0c88155bab.pergunta: At an event with 80 workers without NR-35 training (Serious severity), what is the potential value of the fine?

opcoes.2e8f307f-a69c-43a3-83b6-a13c6f9937af: R$ 3,755.16 (the unit value of the violation)
opcoes.205165d3-1942-4aa6-ba3c-d42e66200df1: R$ 938.78 (minor, since it is the first citation)

f537cb86-369d-4be5-a468-c869204033e9.pergunta: What is the Work Stoppage Notice in the NR-28 system?

opcoes.fa7f4877-471e-4e12-99a8-f8d290daebbf: A notification to the worker about their OSH obligations
opcoes.b60e02dd-f842-4193-97b4-3519f0fc7e55: An instrument that immediately suspends the execution of a construction project or service with serious and imminent risk
opcoes.8889abcd-862f-4b43-9e20-391e8cbee577: A document that imposes an administrative fine for a violation of the NRs
opcoes.1bec4278-d7f6-4c5f-a478-5ccca1020b66: An administrative appeal against an unfavorable decision

3f8592b3-bd6d-4788-a101-adc8f7b6f3a7.pergunta: What is the executive production company's liability regarding OSH violations committed by contracted companies at the event?

opcoes.06914964-5fdf-435b-83cd-e516895addf2: None — each company is responsible only for its own workers
opcoes.87ab8aa4-bcb8-4d7f-8035-97a8c91d69fd: Liability only if there is a written contract with the contractor
opcoes.1d688139-8854-4cf0-8ad3-39c7fbf500fc: Joint or subsidiary liability — the contracting party must verify the compliance of the contractors
opcoes.c04e2811-9809-46f1-85e1-d8881070d75e: Only moral responsibility — with no formal legal implications

347ea4b6-99e5-4fec-aeef-17919be0f099.pergunta: The death of a worker in an accident at an event, caused by the employer's negligence, may constitute which crime in the Penal Code?

opcoes.1ad47c03-1f32-4883-98c3-856bff32a55c: Only an administrative violation — the criminal sphere does not apply to workplace accidents
opcoes.06c03ceb-e5c2-4ae6-8f81-17d936e754e4: Involuntary manslaughter — without intent, through negligence, recklessness or malpractice
opcoes.8520770b-5aad-456c-a388-ea78259c7cb8: Intentional homicide — with the intent to kill
opcoes.df8fd665-2b4a-4044-8a2a-4b3276d8c001: Only negligent bodily harm, never manslaughter

be815beb-5249-446d-8ce3-9e8780395eca.pergunta: What is the causal nexus in civil liability for a workplace accident?

opcoes.989d8b9e-2275-44e9-be65-a3ef4454a347: The police report filed at the station after the accident
opcoes.34191ed9-223d-4789-afa7-a7fae572fa7f: The technical report issued by the SESMT after the accident
opcoes.cddde8f2-90f8-45aa-9f7a-4e0c3f800a74: The employment contract that links the employee to the employer
opcoes.423496b9-016b-463f-bf58-5f568b486f62: The cause-and-effect relationship between the employer's conduct (action or omission) and the harm suffered by the worker

e420b609-77d8-41b2-ba26-f9cafa23e108.pergunta: Which conduct during a labor inspection automatically DOUBLES the fine?

opcoes.32fb7b9d-bfdf-4c88-aca7-292e51363abe: Resistance or contempt toward the labor inspector
opcoes.f1780036-1e2a-463a-99d7-8f6d6717c26f: Asking to see the inspector's official identification
opcoes.f91a2154-5bf3-4998-b1e4-58e8c5aaf321: Requesting additional time to gather the documentation
opcoes.76d44eff-f299-402a-a831-660615db3607: Accompanying the inspection with a lawyer

7139fc83-d9b6-41a9-a8b6-a8bb1969a4a9.pergunta: The company may refuse to sign the Infraction Notice. What happens in this case?

opcoes.caf7ab4f-ffeb-4b54-8519-a434d9d2d843: The inspector documents the refusal and the AI remains fully valid
opcoes.fe3058ee-6365-418e-9c49-efe1f474cf92: The AI loses its validity — without a signature, it has no legal effect
opcoes.36a3ffe4-b950-4e8a-b210-cd0e48f27e91: The fine is cut in half as an incentive to sign
opcoes.1cb5a20b-da93-44b2-b723-6eaeb712a0a9: The AI is canceled and the violation is disregarded

c70a8b2f-1bdb-46b3-80c0-22e23aadd1cf.pergunta: Which document proves that the temporary stage structure was designed and verified by a qualified professional?

opcoes.4a23a28f-16a3-4352-a95d-ae147b07cd3e: An ART (Technical Responsibility Note) issued by a Civil Engineer
opcoes.008420bd-0056-431e-92e3-72a857318825: An updated PGR with the Risk Inventory
opcoes.9438c4a3-adcf-4f1f-be28-b636372e0e2b: An LTCAT prepared by the occupational physician

86fe3df3-f873-46b3-93f7-14e305a93968.pergunta: The AVCB (Fire Department Inspection Certificate) is the responsibility of whom, in relation to an event?

opcoes.9ac1d935-d17e-4d0a-b90d-71791173d049: The venue / location where the event is held — which must keep it valid
opcoes.5eb42523-de63-4955-aeea-932e53b2ed95: The executive production company — which must obtain it before any event
opcoes.d9357ebc-3110-4f32-aef7-abcdea85cefc: The private security company contracted for the event
opcoes.6db1043b-8af0-499b-8316-f51733094640: The OSH technician contracted specifically for the event

9b76484b-f31f-43b1-8649-953a716bacf7.pergunta: What is the minimum retention period for the Hazardous-Activity Report (NR-16) by the employer?

3e889f42-c67d-4957-bb14-7bdfe3b9e55a.pergunta: What is the main objective of NR-26 in the context of live events?
3e889f42-c67d-4957-bb14-7bdfe3b9e55a.explicacao: NR-26 establishes visual safety signaling and identification to communicate risks to all workers, regardless of experience.

opcoes.5b44fd6c-a710-45c1-bcda-810699a2177b: Regulate the use of electronic equipment at events.
opcoes.b272d27f-1591-4a71-aa15-f7ad9ada2b0b: Define the technical procedures for stage assembly.
opcoes.2d1860f5-68d9-4d8f-a66a-1c8d131904a3: Establish safety signaling and identification measures in workplaces.
opcoes.139faffd-35fe-4a91-8c8e-0f5dabb46db8: Ensure the visual aesthetics of the event with harmonious colors.

c91639e7-439c-4fe1-87ce-79a8005cd6e8.pergunta: Why is it important that safety signaling at live events be clear and standardized, according to NR-26?
c91639e7-439c-4fe1-87ce-79a8005cd6e8.explicacao: To prevent signaling from being confused with decorative or scenography elements, ensuring quick identification of risks and safety equipment.

opcoes.7e369a43-a9e1-4e24-ab0b-a8ba9b965678: To match the artistic design of the event.
opcoes.916cf2d1-2e84-42c3-9c39-5397aed37095: So that the audience perceives the safety of the event.
opcoes.d3f44cb2-6b9e-42c4-b4b8-46dcda15d458: To prevent distraction, confusion, and worker fatigue.
opcoes.7a00992e-14bb-403f-b698-2bc05957e44e: To highlight the work of the artists on stage.

82ec0953-d39f-4b22-98c3-feeb6bf0d49e.pergunta: Which NBR 7195 color indicates fire-fighting locations and prohibitions, and how is it applied at live events?
82ec0953-d39f-4b22-98c3-feeb6bf0d49e.explicacao: The color red indicates fire fighting and is used to locate extinguishers, stop buttons, and emergency exits, essential in event environments.

opcoes.88cf5636-c383-4021-98d9-c62166abf5b4: Yellow; applied in fall-risk areas on stages.
opcoes.a9be0e12-5300-4f2e-9e62-e678d03b4ab5: Green; indicates assembly points and first aid.
opcoes.b1981d4a-cd75-47d0-b50f-8161a5f4781a: Blue; used for mandatory PPE-use notices.
opcoes.20087790-7de5-47ed-9d0c-dc4bdd59bc66: Red; applied to extinguishers and emergency exits.

61ffba32-2d22-429b-8732-050f17a93599.pergunta: Considering the use of chemical products at events, which document is mandatory for each product and what is its function?
61ffba32-2d22-429b-8732-050f17a93599.explicacao: The Safety Data Sheet (SDS) is mandatory for each hazardous chemical product and provides information on risks, prevention, and emergency procedures.

opcoes.038d07b5-6500-4e57-80f6-fde2a7a3f4ff: Inspection report; to verify the product's shelf life.
opcoes.6504c309-ed91-44af-b3cd-2b463b460771: Safety Data Sheet (SDS); to communicate risks and safety measures.
opcoes.eecd83e9-0914-41be-b38e-0d371eb2192e: Warranty certificate; to ensure product quality.

fde31e32-e847-4153-9741-379f9c038503.pergunta: According to NR-26, what is the fundamental principle regarding the use of color signaling in the workplace?
fde31e32-e847-4153-9741-379f9c038503.explicacao: Color signaling is an additional layer of protection and does not replace the use of PPE, training, and engineering measures.

opcoes.ebeb1e4d-d11d-46bd-b2a5-26f05aa2386e: Replace the use of PPE in signaled areas.
opcoes.20ce4090-7279-4721-8f94-5fbfa4a240c5: Be used in excess to ensure greater visibility.
opcoes.812d3e69-e788-4c4f-bf4c-ea06b874f6cf: Serve only to decorate the environment.

10376019-63de-4d0d-b5d9-ab5bb08be29b.pergunta: What is the main objective of NR-26 in the live events sector?
10376019-63de-4d0d-b5d9-ab5bb08be29b.explicacao: NR-26 defines safety signaling and identification to visually communicate risks in the workplace, including events.

opcoes.7284da09-57e8-49e4-9641-fdf4dab2e6c7: Regulate only the use of chemical products at events.
opcoes.c7c37182-8eb4-4536-ba67-a228e8cb6184: Establish safety signaling and identification measures to prevent accidents.
opcoes.4a83a594-1e66-4182-b0a3-1db76f8db8fc: Replace the use of PPE with color signaling.
opcoes.cebc9ddb-af24-4ff6-9efa-4c35e4e5a4d1: Define the types of lighting for live events.

f3ae24e7-eae3-40c7-a2be-27baae67a129.pergunta: According to NBR 7195, which color is used to indicate emergency exit locations at live events?
f3ae24e7-eae3-40c7-a2be-27baae67a129.explicacao: Green is standardized to signal safety and emergency, including emergency exits.

9dc1546c-ec89-44c0-9a8e-c6f1ca131ff0.pergunta: Why should the use of colors in signaling be as reduced as possible, especially at live events?
9dc1546c-ec89-44c0-9a8e-c6f1ca131ff0.explicacao: To avoid confusion, distraction, and visual fatigue, ensuring that safety signaling is clear and not confused with scenography.

opcoes.5f9415d9-decd-402a-8364-1e5a6f656ce2: Because many colors can cause distraction and confusion to the worker.
opcoes.acfd47ac-dd7d-4ddd-a3a6-a4b927c21d34: To facilitate the decoration of the event.
opcoes.06cf0bce-7d5e-4491-8e51-87fffb45f81c: To ensure that only authorized personnel see the signaling.

b20ae596-9741-4684-8c4b-c2f7aca3ea17.pergunta: Which of the following colors, according to NR-26 and NBR 7195, indicates the mandatory use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE) at live events?
b20ae596-9741-4684-8c4b-c2f7aca3ea17.explicacao: The color blue indicates obligations and restrictions, such as the mandatory use of PPE.

bc992f0a-af2a-4407-83bb-d10859dc3270.pergunta: At live events, where the artistic production uses many light effects and colors, what is one of the main challenges of safety signaling according to NR-26?
bc992f0a-af2a-4407-83bb-d10859dc3270.explicacao: Signaling must be clear and standardized so as not to be confused with decorative or scenographic elements, ensuring the effectiveness of risk communication.

opcoes.977d2d95-7e27-4929-b665-43d1a3fa8f7f: Ensure that safety signaling is clear and not confused with decoration.
opcoes.f58d8758-d97f-43f7-a2dc-86de04e752fd: Increase the number of colors used to highlight the risks.
opcoes.adf2ed51-c461-477d-a988-aae2160c6e1e: Replace written notices with only colored signaling.
opcoes.ac359fe1-bf4a-4d3d-adb1-ff15333a6803: Avoid the use of any signaling so as not to interfere with the artistic production.

d1d1f4f2-ca0d-4906-b5be-7ba4e7cb0c8e.pergunta: What is the main objective of NR-26 in the live events sector?
d1d1f4f2-ca0d-4906-b5be-7ba4e7cb0c8e.explicacao: NR-26 establishes safety signaling and identification to communicate risks clearly, complementing other preventive measures.

opcoes.07543f51-57d4-4ee4-a7fb-110fa56453e8: Regulate the lighting and sound at live events.
opcoes.62be9336-6f32-44ac-ae29-9e6415123103: Replace the use of PPE and training for the workers.
opcoes.ee399ccc-2980-4042-877a-61b41138d55c: Standardize color signaling and identify chemical product risks.
opcoes.199eb975-5412-44df-9e93-fa5724b62cb5: Determine the artistic and decorative layout of the production.

b365c816-c67e-4111-8c0e-f68a7345c429.pergunta: In the application of NBR 7195 at events, which color indicates the mandatory use of Personal Protective Equipment (PPE)?
b365c816-c67e-4111-8c0e-f68a7345c429.explicacao: The color blue is used to indicate obligations, such as the mandatory use of PPE, according to the NBR 7195 standardization.

0c1623a6-f5f2-4960-9656-dd72131bfd6c.pergunta: Why does NR-26 recommend that the use of colors in signaling be as reduced as possible at live events?
0c1623a6-f5f2-4960-9656-dd72131bfd6c.explicacao: To avoid confusion, distraction, and visual fatigue of the worker, especially in environments with many colors and light effects.

opcoes.1af2e24e-b82b-4f1e-aeca-d5480e568fe8: To ensure that only qualified professionals understand the signaling.
opcoes.62b5f1a5-c1db-43c8-8cda-5122f68f9bf3: So that the artistic production has total freedom in the colors used.
opcoes.00207199-969a-44c1-b8ab-1096e8ec1056: Because colors are expensive to implement.
opcoes.007356f6-f5ba-41e3-8cd4-e86a08b2dd98: To prevent the signaling from being confused with decorative elements.

0a1a298c-3720-4848-9062-055736f8bac2.pergunta: At a live event, which color would you find to signal a fire extinguisher near the generator?
0a1a298c-3720-4848-9062-055736f8bac2.explicacao: Red is the standard color for fire-fighting signaling, such as extinguishers and emergency stop buttons.

007123cd-7da6-4557-93a0-0b786761a0a4.pergunta: Which of the following colors is rarely applied at live events and is related to radiation and nuclear hazards?
007123cd-7da6-4557-93a0-0b786761a0a4.explicacao: The color purple is used to indicate ionizing radiation and radioactive materials, situations uncommon at live events.

opcoes.b28a26c9-4f62-4abc-9c83-fe668b724d40: Safety standard for machines and equipment
opcoes.6f624224-ce83-4560-bdaf-c340ed8d099c: Safety standard for electrical installations and services

b6b6e006-3c13-4871-8afd-cbaf57f81c34.pergunta: From what current level is there a risk of ventricular fibrillation?

6906cb55-fb3f-4612-bca2-72832194be50.pergunta: Why do outdoor events in the rain represent a high electrical risk?

opcoes.4f23f13f-1bd0-4c85-910e-cb4daee4b2c2: Humidity reduces the resistance of the human body, increasing the current in the event of a shock
opcoes.6bc77c2b-5279-4af3-b982-fb7620d70702: Rain changes the class of the electrical fire
opcoes.9624c6bc-e853-4848-b57e-8aa1a4a49f86: Rain causes a short circuit in all equipment

d478c9b7-6e4d-4c2c-8280-02a5f39f5c4f.pergunta: What is the first action in the de-energization sequence according to NR-10?

opcoes.0d192016-96b1-474d-817d-1f5616c5a061: Verification of the absence of voltage
opcoes.5df263b2-11fa-4de8-a9a6-fb91d4ede81b: Application of signaling to prevent re-energization
opcoes.fd8ff1ae-0717-436b-b2e6-09dbb1ddc202: Sectioning — switching off at the switching devices

opcoes.2854e4ee-67ae-4d9f-b99f-4c226a8af079: Lockout and tagout system to prevent re-energization during maintenance
opcoes.a0edbdcb-6389-47d0-ad6d-dcef4bb528ee: A type of industrial electrical connector

7cc74a88-cff7-4dd1-9ed5-2babd93c667a.pergunta: What is the function of the 30mA Residual Current Device (RCD)?

opcoes.87248f4c-31e4-4c69-a4fe-073f54899248: Detect the difference in current between line and neutral and switch off the circuit in case of leakage — protection against shock
opcoes.09fde53d-a0f1-4804-8cc2-19e2fc3f2673: Measure the grounding resistance of the circuit

4a427ecf-bddc-4b83-b2fc-51b90c9f7cca.pergunta: What is the maximum grounding resistance value accepted by NBR 5410 for protection systems?

c0d6fff3-f269-4c35-9a68-967a167626c6.pergunta: Which extinguisher is correct for a fire in ENERGIZED electrical equipment?

opcoes.2eb68523-5acd-48a9-8519-16891dfafb4c: CO₂ (carbon dioxide) or Dry Chemical Powder

7cd59ec7-ed3c-4554-8297-79b952b5f6af.pergunta: What is the Electrical Installations Dossier (PIE)?

opcoes.0a98d5ec-78d1-4ca0-ac3a-ab6e95aa49b4: Contract between the producer and the electrical company
opcoes.bc050be4-a2a9-4af8-bc41-3afae53c07ff: Mandatory technical document for installations with power greater than 75kW, containing the single-line diagram, procedures, training and records
opcoes.5d2cd51b-aa3a-47e8-aee9-8402151cc99c: Operating manual for the electrical equipment
opcoes.870694fa-7c2c-459d-b305-b7ca0eae6f72: Certificate of approval of the electrical PPE

opcoes.c69bfe17-a915-41f0-9c95-39e19ea5785b: Holds a degree in electrical engineering
opcoes.d1fd4db0-63ab-4873-83d9-6a855ec2cf33: Was verbally authorized by the supervisor to do electrical work
opcoes.24099cc9-41b3-448a-9d94-397151b1c776: Is qualified or licensed and received the company's formal consent after NR-10 training
opcoes.9820de29-5514-4f3d-918f-9ff57025d9e7: Has more than 5 years of experience in events

247e2048-fb72-4c9f-b26c-00eab744ab22.pergunta: What is the minimum recommended IP (Ingress Protection) rating for distribution boards at outdoor events?

opcoes.13724c85-ac64-4463-97af-7379619dcd0e: There is no IP requirement for temporary distribution boards

222b5081-5765-4b3c-8669-3992bcddcbab.pergunta: Which type of cable is mandatory for temporary power installations at events?

opcoes.42e65de4-5adc-4866-b8a5-ea3951a97f1b: PP cable (Parallel Polychloroprene) — flexible with rubber sheath

opcoes.ddf096e2-b76b-4281-aee6-3ca19838c0f6: Yes, as long as they are protected by an RCD
opcoes.915e34ba-0671-4780-944e-a1c0bddcb05f: No — residential extension cords do not have adequate current capacity for industrial loads
opcoes.3e4a0c1e-5b7c-464f-b6c0-ae75f5697747: Yes, if the extension cord is of good quality

opcoes.402816b3-2fd5-4cd9-9326-a88eb5af0099: The cable heats up progressively until the insulation melts, potentially causing a fire hours later

d6f3d81b-be72-48e4-a1bf-382d7e23fd94.pergunta: What is the minimum distance of the Risk Zone (RZ) for low-voltage installations (up to 1kV)?

opcoes.31179e37-9b0e-43c4-8ad1-d6fb8fdfb839: 0.20 m — only authorized workers with full PPE may be in this zone

opcoes.a38c18b1-d943-44c2-8f2e-bcdec6e6075d: Curvature of the electrical cable in an installation
opcoes.c9fdf984-63a1-4967-a958-940abd6600a7: High-intensity electrical discharge through the air, with a temperature above 20,000°C

opcoes.d3f2c0b7-853d-4d5a-b958-6a07b5cb1d6b: Apply cold water to the burns
opcoes.7c9b62ed-16de-4454-9c26-b5ea6da438fc: Switch off the power source — never touch the victim while they are in contact with the electricity

900c176d-63d5-4ced-aa41-541051e98088.pergunta: What is the minimum refresher frequency for the NR-10 course?

ce7842b8-c10b-4cb6-b4bd-9a47ff138bb3.pergunta: When does indirect contact with electricity occur?

opcoes.bc3ede30-eff9-48b4-b90a-d539beff8c00: When directly touching an energized conductor
opcoes.a975afda-5282-475e-91ad-edf690415be9: When touching a metal part of equipment that became energized due to an insulation fault

opcoes.954a4bd0-129f-41bb-a6f1-d0049d7f440f: Every metal structure that receives electrical equipment MUST be grounded to the event's earthing system
opcoes.2b8fc1c5-94dd-48c0-be91-7f2b106bfe3d: Grounding is optional for structures smaller than 5 meters
opcoes.fa8c5331-045b-4b2c-88f8-1abe86b0cadd: Grounding is the responsibility of the power utility

opcoes.6168401c-6324-499e-9dbc-44f62c9b3d99: Protective ramp installed over electrical cables that cross areas where people circulate
opcoes.6d163301-8407-49b2-bc51-d88080d77ed8: A type of high-current industrial plug
opcoes.0cb830fc-9879-4ff8-b111-097e909e6445: A special connector for audio signal cables

0a3e5982-ff7e-40ad-ab1f-132490474c7c.pergunta: What is the function of the Preliminary Risk Analysis (APR) in NR-10?

opcoes.219b1d5c-c871-4dec-a7df-b60424815f01: Identify hazards, assess risks and define control measures before any intervention on an electrical installation
opcoes.4373f994-f30e-4ac1-99f2-22cdaace01a7: List the electrical equipment of the event
opcoes.47530fbb-f3fc-4545-95bd-0c265e1576ca: Describe the first-aid procedure for electrical accidents

41fff62d-2ed5-4609-bfe2-0078097375ee.pergunta: Is it correct to make splices in power cables at events?

opcoes.ec8f3335-6d45-4d31-8cfe-52ae21ae9909: Yes, as long as the splice is made with insulating tape and self-fusing tape
opcoes.e6eaa0f2-ce32-4d31-aafc-3510743ad923: Yes, as long as the splice is made with appropriate connectors and adequate technical insulation — never with insulating tape alone
opcoes.a3fa47ea-e555-46c4-ab8f-3394032d3f9b: No — splices in power cables are absolutely prohibited by NR-10
opcoes.f49f6fc5-b2ed-4a31-9b3b-6a9940c215b0: Yes, any splice is acceptable if insulating tape is used

260eb540-b5cc-41a1-b7ed-e8f4236cb687.pergunta: Can a worker with only the 40-hour NR-10 Basic Course work on medium-voltage installations?

opcoes.917b9829-fbd0-4880-a1a8-bc8758824093: Yes, if they have more than 10 years of experience
opcoes.57a17fb1-11d3-464c-8584-25766494057b: No — work on medium voltage requires the complementary SEP course of an additional 40 hours
opcoes.7943e4c9-1410-46c9-95a0-1221069a74a5: Yes, for visual inspection work without contact
opcoes.3ae899d9-cc02-4dda-a0e6-4f37bc343ab3: Yes, with the accompaniment of an engineer

d3eccc93-5fd4-4f0c-a0ed-c5bce54f58dc.pergunta: Why is it prohibited to wear a watch, ring or metal bracelet during electrical work?

opcoes.ce098da6-4367-4b84-9099-43c88b440788: For aesthetic requirements of the work uniform
opcoes.8624fce7-7c11-461d-9123-8a391dd9e180: Because metal objects create a low-resistance path for short-circuit currents, which can cause burns and electric arcs

d0347d66-8f41-4ac0-b11b-969407c2920b.pergunta: The electric generator at events must be positioned:

opcoes.61bfc2af-fdb6-4a9c-b4d1-9b061765e76c: Under the stage to reduce the noise perceived by the production
opcoes.bc74dd33-4252-4dd4-ae27-245efa041895: Near the distribution board to reduce the length of the cables
opcoes.1fe94374-5bed-412b-9dd7-64bf5bfc3749: In any convenient location for the event's logistics
opcoes.1bcc6c10-5999-453a-ba26-bd775aac83e3: In a ventilated location, with grounding, away from combustible materials and the public, in a restricted area

ce69535f-fb1f-4b48-908e-e526da1fd3d0.pergunta: What is the maximum temperature an electric arc can reach?

opcoes.639fed67-a9d7-45ab-bc43-1cf66afc2531: Above 20,000°C — four times the temperature of the sun's surface

9c8eb7dd-157b-4fb2-8ebf-22e7c52cd5fc.pergunta: A victim of electric shock who is conscious and apparently well after the accident:

opcoes.482f7d8a-eb4f-4dfa-9ea1-bcfc99355493: May continue working after a brief rest
opcoes.e5570b5a-6408-427d-b08b-d8261959df06: Does not need medical care if the shock was low-voltage
opcoes.71973531-e524-4c71-927f-b6c85453b56a: Must be observed for 30 minutes and released if no symptoms appear
opcoes.46370129-f5e3-4e14-a5a1-d9f47f198be6: MUST be referred to the hospital for a complete medical evaluation — risk of delayed cardiac arrhythmias and internal injuries

e7e76363-1ae7-4ff1-9e20-b6c7b511af42.pergunta: What is the most common cause of fires of electrical origin at events?

opcoes.54fd036b-1eb2-410f-9db8-82b69ee1201d: Rain that penetrates the distribution boards
opcoes.fed86605-42be-4c25-a57e-543008f952e7: Failure of the residual current device
opcoes.24c37a1a-cc52-4cf8-85da-a94b8f8c13f3: Undersizing of conductors, poor contact in connections and circuit overloads
opcoes.575a9287-5ea0-45e5-b1e7-4085cabbfa0d: Use of equipment more than 5 years old

4d08ba0c-008c-4786-af55-ddc5e724711e.pergunta: What is the main reason for applying NR-18 to live events, such as the assembly of stages and temporary structures?
4d08ba0c-008c-4786-af55-ddc5e724711e.explicacao: NR-18 applies because temporary structures at events present risks similar to those of civil construction, requiring equivalent safety measures.

opcoes.0e56be99-63b3-451f-b6b8-d260137215f1: Because the assembly of structures at events does not involve risks.
opcoes.e7ff6210-8bda-400a-868e-703234910d89: Because events are considered permanent civil works.
opcoes.4324c0ff-ce1f-4cc1-8a2b-af0ed7ee8cc1: Because temporary structures present construction risks similar to those of civil works.
opcoes.faa33f33-6759-48ce-8427-b5380c0e62a6: Because NR-18 is the only standard that governs live events.

e5a74860-8adb-4168-a0c0-5a9da4935326.pergunta: Which document is mandatory to ensure technical responsibility in the assembly of temporary structures at events?
e5a74860-8adb-4168-a0c0-5a9da4935326.explicacao: The ART (Technical Responsibility Note) is fundamental for the legal responsibility of the engineer responsible for the structure.

6f8a71fb-b821-4994-89e0-c7adbf9b5721.pergunta: Why must the occupancy load on bleachers consider dynamic amplification coefficients, such as the 'crowd surfing load'?
6f8a71fb-b821-4994-89e0-c7adbf9b5721.explicacao: Because the rhythmic movement of the public can generate structural resonance, increasing the forces on the structure and the risk of collapse.

opcoes.78c62965-bf8a-497d-9763-32a9ba158665: To account for the weight of the installed equipment.
opcoes.b71f0a65-7d47-4954-9921-acd7e1b374a0: To reduce the number of people allowed on the bleachers.
opcoes.6e09e13a-3fcf-44de-820f-e2e9735d4bdc: To compensate for possible structural calculation errors.
opcoes.b20ed7d2-958c-4501-8c13-bfb1b2ffabcb: Because the movement of the public can amplify the dynamic loads on the structure.

f1f3ad37-914b-49ff-8a31-9d7b1decda67.pergunta: Which standard must be consulted for the calculation of steel structures in bleachers and VIP boxes at events?
f1f3ad37-914b-49ff-8a31-9d7b1decda67.explicacao: ABNT NBR 8681 is the technical standard that establishes requirements and actions for steel structures, including bleachers and VIP boxes.

e88f532b-c9d6-4257-bcb9-6233e807f6ce.pergunta: Regarding the assembly of temporary structures at events with fewer than 20 workers, which document must be prepared to plan safe working conditions?
e88f532b-c9d6-4257-bcb9-6233e807f6ce.explicacao: For smaller teams, the PGR of NR-01 must incorporate the specific features of the temporary structures, replacing the PCMAT required for larger works.

opcoes.c2586508-d61a-4cf5-9968-feeba728d01b: Risk Management Program (PGR) in accordance with NR-01.
opcoes.f4b3282f-6fbb-4c29-a1f0-58a463ef967e: ART of the occupational safety professional.

a20fc861-934b-4b88-8f06-ff49d6e21567.pergunta: What is the main reason for applying NR-18 to temporary assemblies at live events, such as stages and bleachers?
a20fc861-934b-4b88-8f06-ff49d6e21567.explicacao: NR-18 governs the minimum safety conditions in works, and temporary structures at events present risks equivalent to civil works.

opcoes.9019c5d3-bf1e-475d-83d7-b51ec3dffe53: To ensure the aesthetics of the stages and structures.
opcoes.8a872cb5-3298-416a-a351-955ae6f45c11: Because temporary event structures have risks similar to civil works.
opcoes.c70d6ef1-c894-4aff-a8d9-5df10351dd1a: Because NR-18 is specific to live events.
opcoes.35ec72ae-b11f-4b0e-b72f-5e6ce3d18c0d: Due to the exclusive requirement of environmental agencies.

52e10acb-dff0-4061-952a-65f934cc1ec5.pergunta: Which document is mandatory to ensure technical responsibility in the assembly of temporary structures at events?
52e10acb-dff0-4061-952a-65f934cc1ec5.explicacao: The ART registered at CREA proves the technical responsibility of the engineer responsible for the structure.

opcoes.207342b6-ae49-446c-8e9a-7046b33b3aa3: Architectural design approved by the city hall.
opcoes.ca03c151-793e-4f3b-8dcb-3bbb7ec39596: Technical Responsibility Note (ART) registered at CREA.

f9d7e63a-2f2b-4724-8eff-aab3f953f296.pergunta: Regarding the bleachers used at events, which variable floor load is recommended for structural calculation according to ABNT NBR 8681?
f9d7e63a-2f2b-4724-8eff-aab3f953f296.explicacao: The standard suggests 4.0 kN/m2 for occupancy loads, considering the weights and dynamic amplifications of the public.

016e434e-22f9-4fe1-b1fc-2bd9f44a3011.pergunta: Why must the phenomenon known as "crowd surfing load" or "sway load" be considered in the sizing of bleachers?
016e434e-22f9-4fe1-b1fc-2bd9f44a3011.explicacao: Because this phenomenon represents the rhythmic movement of the public that can cause structural resonance and collapse.

opcoes.5564f8e4-a4e8-4741-83c0-056374ed3bda: It increases the resistance of the structure.
opcoes.06f464ae-bf21-460c-988c-77dd55675728: It represents dynamic loads that amplify the forces on the structure.
opcoes.df64b254-2795-4976-b959-ac9edbba52a3: It indicates the vibration caused by sound equipment.
opcoes.3586b183-af96-4aeb-94d5-6d546d625343: It is a static load that can be ignored.

fc0de532-865e-4b49-8b49-0b58cb6f5039.pergunta: What is the relationship between NR-18 and NR-35 in the context of the assembly of temporary structures for events?
fc0de532-865e-4b49-8b49-0b58cb6f5039.explicacao: NR-35 complements NR-18 by specifically regulating work at heights above 2 meters, which is common in structure assembly.

opcoes.208aff1b-7b04-47ae-b3aa-1de2f33efe99: NR-18 deals with lighting and NR-35 with sound.
opcoes.e6d88ef9-0918-4522-a89a-9e603ef87db2: NR-35 regulates work at height, which is part of the risks addressed by NR-18.
opcoes.edafdadc-0d64-4506-892e-7cd22259efcd: There is no relationship between the standards.

2eeedbcb-8d05-48d2-bffd-ad45951438f0.pergunta: Why is NR-18 applicable to the assembly of temporary structures at live events, such as stages and bleachers?
2eeedbcb-8d05-48d2-bffd-ad45951438f0.explicacao: NR-18 governs the minimum safety conditions in temporary works, and event structures present risks similar to those of civil construction.

opcoes.b07117b9-3985-4389-b9c5-17535285b5c8: Because the assembly of these structures constitutes a temporary work subject to NR-18.
opcoes.3efbfd98-d02c-460b-aee0-0daec8f1b68b: Because NR-18 is exclusive to events and not to civil construction.
opcoes.8f0251c5-5442-42f1-9f19-a74e76c3aa1b: Because NR-18 allows ignoring risks at events because they are temporary.
opcoes.3c0a393b-b113-4165-a62d-602562f37983: Because live events are considered permanent works.

520cc626-a506-4ef0-adbf-f05183c67122.pergunta: Which document is mandatory to ensure technical responsibility in the assembly of temporary structures at events, in accordance with NR-18?
520cc626-a506-4ef0-adbf-f05183c67122.explicacao: The ART registered at CREA proves the technical responsibility of the engineer for the assembled structure.

a57882f8-2076-42f5-a8f7-242f3dd632f2.pergunta: In the assembly of stages with an aluminum box-truss structure, what is the main reason for the separate sizing of each structural element?
a57882f8-2076-42f5-a8f7-242f3dd632f2.explicacao: Each element bears specific loads, such as self-weight, occupancy, wind and equipment, requiring an individualized calculation.

opcoes.647e083f-793c-4a4e-8859-67de77a57e3c: To reduce the cost of the materials used.
opcoes.eafb366b-d428-40ca-9191-c5734f56e741: Because each component undergoes different types and intensities of load.
opcoes.58d9b178-0afe-4672-9da4-a881fd7e89f7: To follow a tradition of the events industry.
opcoes.c62c9f13-601f-432d-9072-311ea0cf57d8: To make assembly easier for the workers.

2b0bdbd9-733c-4320-a1ec-cb1a861c0036.pergunta: Considering bleachers for the public, which variable load is adopted for structural calculation according to ABNT NBR 8681 and NR-18?
2b0bdbd9-733c-4320-a1ec-cb1a861c0036.explicacao: The standard variable load for bleachers is 4.0 kN/m2, also considering the dynamic loads of the public in motion.

422c7aa2-ee1e-4e1c-91d9-b0fd3cc9670e.pergunta: What is the relationship between NR-35 and NR-18 in the assembly of temporary structures at live events?
422c7aa2-ee1e-4e1c-91d9-b0fd3cc9670e.explicacao: All work at heights above 2 meters is simultaneously governed by NR-35 and NR-18, ensuring complementary safety.

opcoes.a057a606-b241-49c5-97b9-ea3a5d2c6103: NR-18 regulates height, NR-35 regulates lighting.
opcoes.c8bc6932-be74-4d8f-b166-5502f0edd5b8: Both simultaneously regulate work at heights above 2 meters.
opcoes.41b64c7e-702f-4a04-88b9-945a5015fce5: NR-35 replaces NR-18 in work above 2 meters.
opcoes.5258dc8a-bbcc-4076-87f6-94fe9d3ff18c: NR-35 is applicable only to events and not to constructions.

3510def5-e01c-46f2-a1af-bfd465a6505b.pergunta: What is the definition of work at height according to NR-35?

opcoes.e41c4a2d-e73d-499c-9dc6-56012a5add79: Any activity performed above 2.00m from the lower level, where there is a risk of falling
opcoes.f691cd2b-fc3d-4bdb-aff0-24763cec88af: Activities that require the use of a scaffold or aerial work platform
opcoes.a537e4c4-b6e8-4604-b289-5efec78b37a6: Only work on permanent structures above 3 meters
opcoes.fc2f1f55-e575-4524-8bb6-0587544eb451: Any activity above 5 meters from ground level

8c56dae1-3ce6-4caf-ae05-f6e3dc1fcee0.pergunta: Which type of harness is mandatory for work at height according to NR-35?

opcoes.1ab66047-2cca-48c8-b601-5d3a6908035f: Any harness with a valid CA
opcoes.915dfaff-49cf-4660-85f1-ea5c945d4443: Full-body (parachute-type) harness with a minimum of 5 adjustment points

0bcb8371-d84f-4763-99f5-fa258a793de7.pergunta: What is the double (Y-type) lanyard and when is it recommended?

opcoes.b71f1bc6-dee3-474b-9033-689bd18b8898: A lanyard with two connection legs, recommended for movement on structures — it allows staying connected at all times
opcoes.a38fb3cc-e8fc-44e9-834e-d0c3d3f73573: A lanyard for two workers working together
opcoes.7bfe80fd-380f-4e09-9e44-3cd7086bced3: A lanyard with two energy absorbers, recommended for great heights
opcoes.46e4886a-ecfa-4247-b53d-ccc4304d1474: A spare lanyard in case the main one fails

9dd82c92-ff54-4c24-8d1f-cd6c3c96e7fe.pergunta: Suspension trauma can cause death within how much time?

opcoes.d77db1bd-7d4c-4e0d-895c-661473663944: Only if there is immediate loss of consciousness
opcoes.38acd128-6146-417f-8819-d98690c9d162: Only if there is a traumatic injury from the fall

c9fe625b-e5c3-4448-b031-adb9610a80a0.pergunta: After rescuing a worker who was left suspended in the harness, what is the correct position?

opcoes.592f0f81-9fa2-46c4-a508-b0ca0a973f82: Standing supported to force venous return
opcoes.f12dbddb-edeb-4d44-b392-a2445e84d979: Semi-seated with knees bent for 20-30 minutes — do NOT lay down immediately

88274032-86c6-4eff-aa94-a2480ee1fdcf.pergunta: What is the minimum load that an anchorage point for work at height must withstand?

8c90de1f-9300-4377-9f9e-df312f4f8c74.pergunta: Work at height must be suspended when the wind speed exceeds:

94fb92a8-1068-466f-978d-328a3d75f365.pergunta: What is the Fall Factor (FF) and which is the most dangerous situation?

opcoes.21f59af4-2fc6-445a-a3e5-d15a90bab40f: The ratio between the free-fall distance and the lanyard length — FF 2 is the most dangerous
opcoes.750980c1-9310-454a-971b-99eb908c89f0: The ratio between the working height and the number of anchorage points — a high FF is safer
opcoes.80fb2b18-5db3-4c61-a3f4-f77473f4e8f1: The load amplification factor at the anchorage point — FF 1 is the safest
opcoes.e2e537d1-7457-4a1a-b9fc-d866e3e2225c: The ratio between the worker's weight and the harness capacity — FF 0 is the most dangerous

6c13a323-6f9c-4135-85fa-dc5330146444.pergunta: When is the lanyard's energy absorber mandatory?

opcoes.e7e4b324-786e-488f-a283-5f1085686914: When the lanyard length exceeds 0.9m or when the fall factor is greater than 1
opcoes.dedfca81-8cd8-4f19-a06f-499ba925495d: Only when the ground below is concrete

ca7684da-e1c6-4185-b6d9-14174882fb12.pergunta: What is the Work Permit (WP) and when must it be issued?

89d008ef-f8e6-4072-b5bc-ceb67b3cf82b.pergunta: Why does the live events sector present a fire risk considered extraordinarily serious?
89d008ef-f8e6-4072-b5bc-ceb67b3cf82b.explicacao: The risk is high due to the combination of combustible materials, powerful ignition sources, a large concentration of audience, and often inadequate evacuation routes.

opcoes.6c327907-109c-471f-848b-e698dc851a95: Because of the weather conditions during the event.
opcoes.6f8802ae-1abd-4e31-8d82-75983739122c: Because they use only highly flammable materials.
opcoes.a7b3e136-4aa8-477d-b559-e3575616019c: Due to the combination of combustible materials, ignition sources, and audience concentration with often inadequate evacuation routes.
opcoes.0026d7d8-756c-4dbf-8180-ab05dbfacb2a: Because they do not have a trained fire brigade.

ae15e735-0f8e-4a3a-85e6-5a4e178b2a6e.pergunta: What is the main reason for not using water to extinguish Class C fires at live events?
ae15e735-0f8e-4a3a-85e6-5a4e178b2a6e.explicacao: Water conducts electricity and can cause an electric shock to the firefighter in fires with energized Class C equipment.

opcoes.0ecbee64-d71e-42f8-ae20-b0e281cd9730: Water is not effective against flammable liquids.
opcoes.30e83166-1872-4af0-9244-42e1c66754f2: Water is prohibited by current legislation.
opcoes.3b2f7bc7-545a-473d-9434-4ec84d361e2d: Water can put out the fire, but leaves residue.
opcoes.44dc9619-9f71-4bdc-b359-aa2c192eee7f: Water can cause an electric shock by conducting electric current through the jet to the firefighter.

b89d48d2-98c1-4659-b83c-4c0681ad6bc4.pergunta: After the Kiss Nightclub fire, which legislation established national guidelines for fire safety at public-assembly events?
b89d48d2-98c1-4659-b83c-4c0681ad6bc4.explicacao: Federal Law No. 13,425/2017, known as the Kiss Law, was created to establish these national guidelines.

6673041c-9940-4955-bd6a-bef00f3de326.pergunta: Which extinguishing agent is recommended for Class B fires at live events, and why?
6673041c-9940-4955-bd6a-bef00f3de326.explicacao: Extinguishers with BC powder, ABC powder, CO₂, or mechanical foam are recommended because they do not spread the flammable liquid and put out the fire at the surface.

opcoes.c3cf6fd3-d1f6-4500-8920-f3aa8f4a3087: BC powder, ABC powder, CO₂, or mechanical foam, because they avoid spreading flammable liquids.
opcoes.b9f31c43-a7dc-47b1-a09d-3ba2aa8aa60e: D powder, because it is specific for combustible metals.
opcoes.6d7f5b9f-0358-4b8a-912c-727cc7de28c5: Class K wet agent, recommended for fats.
opcoes.a91abbab-bf9f-4ce4-8417-f962f8931602: Pressurized water, because it penetrates the material well.

74480f16-cb55-4d53-8970-5d82469106f6.pergunta: According to NR-23, what is the legal consequence of failing to comply with fire protection measures at public-assembly events?
74480f16-cb55-4d53-8970-5d82469106f6.explicacao: Non-compliance may constitute a criminal offense for exposing third parties to risk of life and strict civil liability in the event of an accident.

opcoes.4e3107b7-7043-4b3b-a23b-0e33d417566b: Suspension of the event without legal penalties.
opcoes.6678d4fc-5768-4a81-bb46-dafc83cde6fd: No legal consequence, only technical guidance.
opcoes.278e2357-aac2-4941-a417-2da87bfe7c0f: Only an administrative fine for the organizer.
opcoes.b104f7ba-2257-4925-abae-03d85bcb3539: Civil and criminal liability, including exposure to risk of life and accidents.

10066693-01a0-4b03-b00a-a0e8002cf5df.pergunta: Why is the fire risk at live events considered of extraordinary seriousness according to NR-23?
10066693-01a0-4b03-b00a-a0e8002cf5df.explicacao: The combination of combustible materials, powerful ignition sources, a numerous audience, and limited evacuation routes significantly increases the risk.

opcoes.4cfaca74-abf2-4619-b524-f106ad7db125: Because of the high load of combustible materials, powerful ignition sources, a large audience, and restricted evacuation routes.
opcoes.7a9b2743-c110-43f5-a301-f3d790ec1138: Due to the quantity of electronic equipment used.
opcoes.a4591ccb-43b2-48b2-a2dc-90fd46d8295e: Because the events are held in open and ventilated venues.
opcoes.85dee676-862c-4842-963d-1491e196986c: Because live events do not follow specific technical standards.

7571d723-a195-446a-83ab-a8dbf07b1a8d.pergunta: Which extinguishing agent is recommended for fires in energized electrical equipment (Class C) at live events?
7571d723-a195-446a-83ab-a8dbf07b1a8d.explicacao: For electrical fires, it is essential to use non-conductive agents such as CO₂ or BC/ABC powder to avoid electric shock.

eb3567f8-e372-46ce-b60c-42410455eb67.pergunta: According to the Kiss Law and NR-23, who is responsible for fire safety at live events in Brazil?
eb3567f8-e372-46ce-b60c-42410455eb67.explicacao: The responsibility is personal and direct on the organizer, even if the implementation is delegated to third parties.

opcoes.ec242f79-1a6f-428f-a296-c17d11255587: The event organizers, regardless of delegation
opcoes.708d0d93-bb22-44c6-938e-fb3cee307994: The audience present at the event

4e05b234-d515-48a7-b2f2-97f4dc12a31e.pergunta: What is the fatal error when fighting Class C fires at live events, and why?
4e05b234-d515-48a7-b2f2-97f4dc12a31e.explicacao: Using water on electrical fires can conduct electricity, causing an electric shock to the firefighter.

opcoes.d98332f5-8f3b-4fac-9ddc-47d162e60260: Using dry sand, because it can worsen the fire
opcoes.b285f046-0e48-4eb7-a462-2487f84a7f62: Using water, because it conducts electricity and poses a shock risk
opcoes.ac16f9a3-2fbf-42dd-bde3-2fa7c4a9e66c: Using CO₂, because it can cause poisoning
opcoes.b5cfc95a-0d07-402e-b8f6-b3dea15d2a71: Using ABC powder, because it is not efficient in class C

02536c90-9b35-486e-9e66-21a43ec23f05.pergunta: Which ABNT standard specifies the requirements and procedures for fire brigades at live events?
02536c90-9b35-486e-9e66-21a43ec23f05.explicacao: NBR 14276 establishes the requirements for fire and emergency brigades, being fundamental for events.

55a86efa-17fb-497a-9ef5-3a142dda2e4f.pergunta: Why is the fire risk at live events considered of high seriousness according to NR-23?
55a86efa-17fb-497a-9ef5-3a142dda2e4f.explicacao: The combination of combustible materials, powerful ignition sources, a large audience, and precarious evacuation routes makes the risk more serious.

opcoes.224ee59c-43fa-47a0-9e30-e80d95ac2576: Because of the organizers' scant attention to the ABNT standards at live events.
opcoes.6400c80a-4b75-4871-88ca-db8ad94d6138: Because live events usually take place outdoors, facilitating the spread of fire.
opcoes.b39d2c48-80e9-4f7b-a63a-928ab8a143f6: Due to the high concentration of combustible materials, powerful ignition sources, a large audience, and often inadequate evacuation routes.
opcoes.2050a672-dcc7-45b3-a60e-674f7db29c56: Because most events use only energized electrical equipment, which is highly flammable.

24131c97-18d7-4994-ac76-ae3266070377.pergunta: Which extinguishing agent is suitable for Class B fires at live events, and why?
24131c97-18d7-4994-ac76-ae3266070377.explicacao: BC powder, ABC powder, CO₂, or mechanical foam extinguishers are recommended, since water can spread the flammable liquid.

opcoes.8c12f175-9186-49e9-b1a8-b1fdbab1aaba: BC powder, ABC powder, CO₂, or mechanical foam extinguishers, because they do not spread flammable liquids.
opcoes.f25c63e2-b43c-4637-85dc-9354961b662d: Class K wet agent, because it is efficient for fats.
opcoes.2e0b7262-7d06-466d-968c-9a8e952f8f11: Pressurized water, because it penetrates the fire better.
opcoes.9d4e44dd-2119-4910-ad40-2eeb76ef7e60: Specific D powder, because it is recommended for combustible metals.

a2248818-b398-4d57-8702-b06372d319f8.pergunta: What is the consequence of using water to put out a fire in energized electrical equipment (Class C)?
a2248818-b398-4d57-8702-b06372d319f8.explicacao: Water conducts electricity and can cause a fatal electric shock to the firefighter.

opcoes.53819a8c-41e6-4c38-bea0-f5ef731825bc: Water conducts electricity and can cause a potentially fatal electric shock.
opcoes.33649ae6-0bf6-4ef7-af18-c810b0eb88eb: Water puts out the fire quickly without risks.
opcoes.a8cbb470-7306-4dc5-9201-6b12e804319c: Water can react with the metal and intensify the fire.
opcoes.f247a520-4f6b-4050-8a7f-a9ed964dd6b9: Water neutralizes the toxic gases released during the fire.

ad2881e6-a199-429c-a09f-dd548d2208bf.pergunta: According to the Kiss Law (Law No. 13,425/2017), what is the responsibility of event organizers regarding fire safety?
ad2881e6-a199-429c-a09f-dd548d2208bf.explicacao: Organizers are personally responsible for the safety of the public, even if they delegate the implementation of the measures to third parties.

opcoes.aff8cbda-96ed-4e1c-8488-610f7d7e4b10: They are responsible only if they are present during the event.
opcoes.4956af28-d89b-4965-9429-cff58c6ddc9d: They must know and apply the guidelines, being personally responsible for the safety of the public.
opcoes.a3f8baac-9aa3-41ee-b341-642f206806e9: The responsibility rests exclusively with the Fire Brigades during the event.
opcoes.1fc9f799-67e6-4eb8-af16-c1e36060d5c0: They may delegate the responsibility and not be personally liable in the event of an accident.

7df3b7ab-527d-48a9-9c8f-b3961da15679.pergunta: Which ABNT standard establishes the requirements for the fire brigade at live events?
7df3b7ab-527d-48a9-9c8f-b3961da15679.explicacao: ABNT NBR 14276:2020 defines the requirements and procedures for the fire and emergency brigade.

79c6abe1-9830-49e5-9180-405fa20e950a.explicacao: Load handling at events involves large volumes and risks due to factors such as weight, a temporary environment, deadlines and multiple teams.
